questions.text: What does the MLFQ scheduler use to determine a process's priority?
questions.explanation: The MLFQ adjusts priority based on observed behavior: CPU-bound processes get demoted, I/O-bound processes stay high priority.

questions.text: Which system call creates a new process in Unix/Linux?
questions.explanation: fork() duplicates the calling process. clone() is Linux-specific with finer sharing control.

questions.text: Which of the following are valid page replacement algorithms?
questions.explanation: LRU, FIFO, and Optimal are page replacement algorithms. FCFS is a CPU scheduling algorithm.

options.A: When two processes compete for CPU time
options.B: When the outcome depends on the timing of uncontrollable events
options.C: When a process exceeds its memory limit
options.D: When a deadlock occurs between three or more processes

questions.explanation: A race condition occurs when behavior depends on the timing of uncontrollable events.

questions.text: In virtual memory, what does the TLB cache?
questions.explanation: The TLB caches recent virtual-to-physical address mappings.
